topicDefinition.oneLiner: Enterprise-grade AI video solutions for corporate training, marketing, and professional content creation.
boundaries: Must offer business or enterprise-tier features like team collaboration, SSO/SAML, or brand kit governance.
Must support high-quality commercial export (1080p/4K) and integrations (LMS/SCORM, APIs).
Excludes consumer-focused apps with strict commercial use limits or no team administration capabilities.

howToChoose.title: Check Governance and Compliance
howToChoose.desc: If you are deploying across a large organization, verify if the tool supports Single Sign-On (SSO) and role-based access control (RBAC).

howToChoose.title: Evaluate Localization Capabilities
howToChoose.desc: For global teams, look for platforms that offer 1-click translation and dubbing natively, rather than requiring third-party tools.

howToChoose.title: Review Export Workflows
howToChoose.desc: If building training materials, prioritize tools that export to SCORM formats directly to your Learning Management System (LMS).

howToChoose.title: Assess Brand Management
howToChoose.desc: A good enterprise tool must allow you to lock down fonts, colors, and logos across the entire team to maintain brand consistency.

sources.title: Best AI Video Generators for Business in 2026
sources.excerpt: A breakdown of top AI video platforms focusing on enterprise production, corporate training, and team collaboration.

sections.heading: Top Enterprise Platforms
sections.content: Visla stands out for full-stack enterprise production, offering creation, editing, collaboration, and brand management. Synthesia specializes in corporate training and internal communications with highly realistic AI avatars. Pictory excels at repurposing existing content into video with SSO/SAML.

keyClaims: Offers creation, editing, collaboration, and brand management in one platform
Includes AI avatars, voice cloning, and screen recording
proofSnippets: Visla stands out for full-stack enterprise production, offering creation, editing, collaboration, and brand management in one platform with plans starting at $18/mo.

keyClaims: Highly realistic AI avatars in 160+ languages
Supports real-time collaboration and LMS exports
proofSnippets: Synthesia specializes in corporate training and internal communications with highly realistic AI avatars in 160+ languages, starting at $29/mo.

keyClaims: Enterprise features including SSO/SAML, comprehensive brand kits, and AI avatars
proofSnippets: Pictory excels at repurposing existing content into video, with enterprise features including SSO/SAML, comprehensive brand kits, AI avatars, and a robust API for automation at $29/mo.

notes: Focuses directly on business value and enterprise scalability features.

sources.title: Synthesia Official Pricing and Enterprise Features
sources.excerpt: Official details on Synthesia's enterprise tier, SSO, and SCORM support.

sections.heading: Enterprise Pricing
sections.content: Custom enterprise pricing with unlimited video minutes and custom credit allocation.

sections.heading: Enterprise Features
sections.content: SAML/SSO is exclusively available on the Enterprise plan. Includes SCORM export capability on the Enterprise plan only.

keyClaims: Unlimited video minutes on custom Enterprise plans
pricingClaims: Custom enterprise pricing required for full suite
exportRules: SCORM export capability is strictly locked to the Enterprise plan
Video downloads restricted on lower plans; unlocked at Creator tier ($89/month)
limitations: SAML/SSO is completely unavailable on Free, Starter, or Creator tiers
proofSnippets: SAML/SSO is exclusively available on the Enterprise plan; it's not included in Free, Starter, or Creator tiers.
Synthesia includes SCORM export capability on the Enterprise plan only.

notes: Highly accurate limitation data pulled directly from pricing comparisons.

sources.title: Visla Official Pricing and Enterprise Guidelines
sources.excerpt: Official documentation on Visla credits, SSO, and export resolutions.

sections.heading: Pricing Logic
sections.content: Enterprise plans start from 20,000 monthly credits. Text-based projects use 1 credit per second; voice-based uses 1.5x.

sections.heading: Enterprise Features
sections.content: Single sign-on (SSO) is available on Business and Enterprise plans. Supports resolutions up to 4K.

keyClaims: Enterprise API access for workflow integration
Task-based credit system based on video generation types
pricingClaims: Enterprise plans start from 20,000 monthly credits
exportRules: Supports resolutions up to 4K on Business and Enterprise plans
limitations: Voice-based projects consume 1.5x more credits than text-based ones
Single sign-on (SSO) is restricted to Business and Enterprise plans
proofSnippets: Single sign-on (SSO) is available on Visla's Business and Enterprise plans, but not on lower tiers.
Visla supports multiple video export options and resolutions up to 4K on Business and Enterprise plans.

notes: Accurate reflection of B2B credit consumption mechanics.

sources.title: Pictory Enterprise Capabilities
sources.excerpt: Details regarding Pictory's minimum seat requirements and SCORM support.

sections.heading: Enterprise Requirements
sections.content: Custom pricing requires a 10+ users minimum. Includes custom storage, video minutes, and AI credits.

keyClaims: Interactive video hosting with clickable CTAs and quizzes (Pictory Central)
Unlimited brand kits and custom AI voices
exportRules: SCORM export (LMS Ready) is included as an Enterprise feature
limitations: Enterprise tier enforces a strict 10+ users minimum seat count
proofSnippets: SCORM export (LMS Ready) is included as an Enterprise feature, enabling videos to be exported as SCORM packages for learning management systems.

notes: Clear boundaries on team size minimums.
